issue.weather: Cloudy w/ chance of patch notes

ticker.text: Is Capcom having the greatest single year in gaming history? The case is strong
Masters of Albion early access — Peter Molyneux's comeback is real
Tides of Tomorrow out now — 77 OpenCritic, beautiful but divisive
Diablo 4: Lord of Hatred drops April 28 — one week out preview
Forza Horizon 6 Japan — May 19 launch, day one Game Pass

lead.title: Is Capcom Having the Greatest Single Year in Gaming History?
lead.dek: Resident Evil Requiem, Monster Hunter Stories 3, Pragmata — three games in the top 10 highest-rated of 2026, and Onimusha is still coming. The question has stopped being rhetorical.
lead.author: Romello Morris

poll.question: Which remaining April release are you most locked in for?

calendar.title: Kiln
calendar.sub: Full Launch · Day One Game Pass

calendar.title: Saros
calendar.sub: PS5 Exclusive · Deluxe Early Access Apr 28

spotlight.title: Peter Molyneux Is Back — And Masters of Albion Might Actually Be Good
spotlight.blurb: Masters of Albion launched into Early Access today at $24.99. Build by day, defend by night — and yes, you can kick chickens. After years of overpromising, early impressions suggest the redemption arc is real.

comments.name: Marcus T.
comments.body: Capcom has been cooking for three years straight. Requiem, Stories 3, Pragmata, and Onimusha still on deck. If Onimusha is a 9+ this is genuinely the greatest single publisher year in history.

comments.name: Priya S.
comments.body: Vampire Crawlers day 2 and the community already broke it. The Turboturn speed-run builds are unhinged. This is exactly what happened with Vampire Survivors. Poncle cannot miss.

comments.name: Jade W.
comments.body: Tides of Tomorrow is divisive but I'm firmly in the 'loved it' camp. The Online Story-Link system is genuinely affecting when it works. Digixart are doing things nobody else is trying.

comments.name: Devon K.
comments.body: GTA 6 Trailer 3 analysis is exactly what I needed. The marketing silence since Trailer 2 has been painful — Take-Two earnings call in May feels like the unlock moment.
